ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Deposit Of Funds Into Blocked Account
Off Calendar Mar-19-2025 Continued To Apr-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Administrator Xi Yan Ruan and Attorney Eileen Magtibay to appear on Apr-16-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why Receipts for Deposits in to Blocked accounts have not been filed. An Order to Show Cause as to why the court should not sanction Attorney Eileen Magtibay in the amount of $250 for failure to appear is set for Apr-16-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-06-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eileen Magtibay for Xi Yan Ruan, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Chia-Chen Erin Shih for Kenneth Ruan and Jacquline Ruan, Heirs (via Zoom).

Ms. Magtibay stated that she had nothing to add and submitted on her papers. No objections were raised. The court took the matter under submission with intent to grant. A compliance status hearing was set for March 19, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, regarding the filing of acknowledgment of receipt of order and funds for deposit in blocked account.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eileen Magtibay, via ZOOM, for Xi Yan Ruan, Petitioner; Vinod Nichani, for Kenneth Ruan and Jacqueline Ruan, Minor Beneficiaries.

The court advises that it would like to address the issue of notice to minors Kenneth and Jacqueline. Ms. Magtibay reports that notice was given to the minors and advises that Mr. Nichani may be willing to waive notice on behalf of his clients. Mr. Nichani confirms that he is waiving notice on behalf of his clients and the court accepts. The court then points to the waivers of bond filed by the mothers of the minors and notes that there is no Guardian Ad Litem to advocate on behalf of the minors and advises that a Guardian Ad Litem will need to be appointed if monetary distributions are made to the minors. Ms. Magtibay asks the court to consider the waivers of bond as she doesn't believe that there are any objections to doing so and that counsel will work on petitioning the court to appoint a Guardian Ad Litem before any distributions are made. The court advises counsel that it is waiving the bond issue but that it will be expecting a petition for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem which can be done via a drop-off ex parte application.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is submitted. Status on the Appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem is set for Dec-13-2021 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
